food_name: Martabak Manis (Indonesian Sweet Thick Pancake — Classic Peanut-Coconut Filling)
food_subgroup: Thick yeast-leavened sweet pancake with peanut-coconut filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Martabak Manis. Closest parent is thick leavened sweet pancake with coconut-peanut filling. No exact FoodOn URI available; null is appropriate.
ontology.foodex2_description: Thick yeast-leavened sweet pancake with peanut-coconut filling (Indonesian martabak manis style)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CEB used as closest parent for leavened sweet pancake; no exact martabak manis code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Martabak Manis or equivalent Indonesian sweet pancake.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one quarter of a full martabak manis, weighing about 120 g, which is the typical portion sold at Indonesian street stalls and night markets.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Manis (classic peanut-coconut filling) — estimated from component ingredients using FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-reference
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Martabak Manis as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated from component ingredients (wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, grated coconut, peanuts) using FAO/INFOODS SEA Table as the primary regional reference and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as secondary cross-reference. Values are consistent with the input record which was derived from the same FAO/INFOODS SEA source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white wheat flour-based leavened pancake products — University of Sydney GI Database; GI estimated at ~78 driven by white wheat flour batter (GI 75–80) and granulated sugar filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Martabak Manis in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 78 is estimated from the closest analogous food: white wheat flour-based leavened pancake (GI ~75–80) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted for the high granulated sugar filling content. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (78 × 46.5 × 1.20) / 100 = 43.6 g. This is consistent with the input record value.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 280 mg/100 g; a 120 g serving delivers approximately 336 mg sodium from butter, margarine, and batter salt
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one quarter serving (60 g) to keep sodium intake below 170 mg per occasion, and should avoid versions made with salted margarine.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic index (~78) and very high glycemic load per serving (43.6) from white wheat flour batter and generous granulated sugar filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Martabak Manis due to its very high glycemic load of 43.6 per standard 120 g serving, which will cause a large postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, portion should be restricted to no more than 60 g and paired with a protein or fat source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (7.5 g/100 g) from butter and grated coconut, plus trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) from margarine if used, and high sugar (22 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Martabak Manis to occasional small portions (maximum 60 g) due to its high saturated fat content of 7.5 g/100 g, presence of trans fat from margarine, and high sugar load, all of which are unfavourable for lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Proinflammatory combination of saturated fat (7.5 g/100 g), high sugar (22 g/100 g), high GI, and trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) from margarine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Martabak Manis to one small portion (60 g) on special occasions only, as the combination of butter saturated fat, high sugar, high GI, and possible trans fat from margarine is unfavourable for inflammatory conditions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak Manis (also called Terang Bulan in Java, or Martabak Bangka by origin) is one of Indonesia's most beloved street food desserts, widely sold at night markets, roadside stalls, and Ramadan bazaars across the archipelago. It is a thick, fluffy, crater-topped sweet pancake cooked in a large, heavy cast-iron pan. The yeast-leavened batter — made from wheat flour, eggs, yeast, baking soda, sugar, and a pinch of salt — is poured into the hot greased pan and covered to cook until the base is golden-brown and the top surface is covered with characteristic bubbles and craters. Before folding in half, the spongy interior is generously loaded with melted butter, granulated sugar, freshly grated coconut, and crushed roasted peanuts in the classic version. The folded pancake is then sliced into rectangular portions and served immediately while hot. The result is an extraordinarily rich, sweet, eggy dessert that is energy-dense (355 kcal/100 g), very high in sugar (22 g/100 g), and moderately high in fat (16.5 g/100 g) including saturated fat (7.5 g/100 g) from butter and coconut. The glycemic index is high (~78) and the glycemic load per standard 120 g serving is very high at 43.6, making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease in regular or large portions. This record covers the classic peanut-coconut version only — not the modern chocolate, Nutella, cheese, or condensed milk variants. Martabak Manis has Betawi and Arab-Indonesian culinary roots and is considered a national street food icon of Indonesia.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients using FAO/INFOODS SEA Table and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as cross-reference, as no single approved-source entry exists for Martabak Manis as a composite dish. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on white wheat flour leavened pancake. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact dish-level measurement from an approved primary source is available.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Martabak Manis as a composite dish are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA Table or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the consulted approved sources. trans_fat_g of 0.2 g/100 g is present from margarine if used in preparation; this is a conditional value depending on whether butter or margarine is used by the vendor. All core macros (protein_g 7.8, fat_g 16.5, carbohydrate_g 46.5) are non-zero and well-supported by component estimation. GI of 78 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on white wheat flour leavened pancake (GI 75–80); no direct Martabak Manis GI measurement exists in the database. This record covers the classic peanut-coconut filling version only.
